summary: Backend architecture keeps policy, use-case orchestration, interface adapters, and infrastructure details separate so dependency direction remains inward.
title: Core backend architecture principles

summary: Repository abstractions are a concrete way to invert persistence dependencies while keeping infrastructure implementations outside policy code.
title: Dependency inversion and repository boundary

summary: Application services coordinate use cases through domain policy and ports while avoiding transport and database mechanics.
title: Service and use-case boundary

summary: Structural refactors and behavior changes should remain distinguishable when mixing them would raise review, rollback, or reasoning cost.
title: Structure and behavior workflow

summary: Coupling increases change cost, decoupling must be economically justified, and cohesion guides where boundaries should be placed.
title: Coupling, cohesion, and change cost

summary: Under uncertainty, prefer small reversible structure changes that preserve options and reduce regret before broad rewrites.
title: Reversible architecture changes

summary: FastAPI routers map to interface adapters, response models map to DTO/schema boundaries, and SQLAlchemy belongs behind infrastructure ports.
title: FastAPI framework adapter

summary: FastAPI Depends is useful for dependency wiring, but using it inside application services can leak framework APIs across architecture boundaries.
title: FastAPI Depends layer leak risk

summary: Spring controllers map to interface adapters, services map to application coordination, repositories map to persistence boundaries, and JPA belongs in infrastructure.
title: Spring framework adapter

summary: OpenAPI generated contracts shape DTO/schema boundaries, while Spring Security filters, JWT, OAuth2, roles, CORS, and CSRF stay at security/interface boundaries.
title: Spring API contract and security boundaries

summary: WebFlux, Reactive Streams, Mono, Flux, and R2DBC support non-blocking adapters, but reactive framework types should be contained unless the architecture intentionally exposes them.
title: Spring reactive boundary

summary: API contracts, data modeling, and data-layer patterns turn abstractions, schemas, transactions, repositories, ORM behavior, CQRS, and migrations into explicit backend boundaries.
title: Python architecture boundaries and data

summary: Twelve-Factor runtime concerns, web server structure, event-driven queues, and monolith/microservice choices should be evaluated by boundary clarity and operational cost.
title: Runtime, event flow, and service shape tradeoffs

summary: Testing, TDD, packaging, logging, metrics, profiling, debugging, and continuous architecture provide evidence and feedback for incremental backend evolution.
title: Quality evidence and continuous architecture

summary: DDD adds business-value and language evidence to backend architecture by classifying subdomains and drawing bounded contexts before implementation patterns are chosen.
title: DDD strategic boundaries

summary: Context maps, anticorruption layers, shared kernels, open host services, and conformist relationships make cross-boundary integration risk explicit.
title: DDD context integration patterns

summary: Transaction script and active record fit simpler domains, while entities, value objects, aggregates, repositories, and domain events support richer invariant-heavy models.
title: DDD tactical modeling boundaries

summary: Event sourcing, CQRS, event storming, event-driven architecture, microservice boundaries, and data mesh should be selected by domain value, consistency, ownership, and operational cost.
title: DDD event and data architecture

summary: Optional, Stream, and CompletableFuture improve Spring/backend code only when absence, side effects, executor ownership, timeout, and failure behavior stay explicit at architecture boundaries.
title: Modern Java backend quality boundaries

summary: The meta graph now connects public-safe source additions about FastAPI runtime setup, Spring API risk/observability, and Tidy First reversible-change economics into backend architecture guidance.
title: Source-grounded runtime, risk, and change economics

summary: 원문 내용과 개념을 사용하되 표현 복제, 검색 대체물화, tracked private source 유출을 막는 backend-level governance다.
title: Public-safe extraction governance

summary: coverage matrix, graph density, query QA를 함께 써서 얇은 그래프와 과도한 public summary를 동시에 피한다.
title: Coverage, density, query gates

summary: same_as, closeMatch, maps_to, specializes를 분리해 repository, boundary, adapter 같은 cross-skill 용어를 안전하게 연결한다.
title: Canonical cross-skill mapping

summary: reviewed source는 normal lane으로, needs_review나 unreviewed source는 summary-only limited lane으로 처리한다.
title: Source quality lanes

summary: backend meta graph로 승격된 node마다 근거 source, 독립 source 수, adapter rationale, limitation을 남긴다.
title: Backend meta promotion records

summary: batch 생성 후 lint, audit, verbatim, sample review, 개선, 재검증, 2차 리뷰를 반복하는 실행 loop다.
title: Review improve review loop

summary: 여러 batch가 누적되어 원문의 목차와 핵심 설명을 대체하지 않도록 market substitute와 heart-of-work risk를 추적한다.
title: Market substitute risk controls

summary: backend-architecture는 raw OCR이 아니라 source skill의 public graph ID만 사용해 cross-domain backend 판단을 만든다.
title: Public graph only meta synthesis
